content: I found that Box<[T], A>(in rust/alloc/boxed.rs) does not implement the two functions: try_new_uninit_slice and try_new_zeroed_slice, but they are both implemented for Box<[T]>. So should these two functions be implemented for Box<[T], A>?
No, because that'd require an allocator, and should be named try_new_uninit_slice_in/try_new_zeroed_slice_in

Yes, I misnamed the functions, they should be try_new_uninit_slice_in, try_new_zeroed_slice_in respectively. Should these two functions be implemented? Shouldn't the allocator be alloc: A in the function parameters?
Only if there is an expected user in-tree. Or are you asking about upstream alloc?

No. The situation is like this, I now need to create a Box using try_new_uninit_slice and try_new_zeroed_slice in Box<[T], A>, because I turned on the no_global_oom_handling config. But these two functions are not implemented in Box<[T], A>. So I can't use any of the functions provided in Box<[T], A>.

because I turned on the no_global_oom_handling config

What do you mean? That is something that Rust for Linux uses in alloc, but you should not need to "turn it on", i.e. it is already done for you.

I think we are missing some information here -- where do you want to use those functions?

Our team is writing a driver and plans to open source it at the end of this month (around the 28th). This driver needs to turn on the no_global_oom_handling config, so it needs to implement the two functions try_new_uninit_slice_in and try_new_zeroed_slice_in in Box<[T], A>.

That is great news!

If the driver is intended to be upstreamed to the mainline Linux kernel, then there should be no problem adding the functions when you upstream the driver itself. If not, we may not be able to add them until there is a use case in-tree, but you could still propose them to Rust's alloc and we may eventually get them in the kernel.

In any case, I am not sure I understand what you are doing, because you should not need to do anything regarding no_global_oom_handling. Where are you "turning it on"?

There is a mistake in my statement. In RFL, the no_global_oom_handling config should be opened originally, not by us.
And our team hopes to push the driver we wrote into the mainline, but because it relies on the dovetail patch, the changes to native Linux are a bit large, so we are not sure whether it can be integrated into the mainline. So we are going to push this patch to the rust alloc crate.
Thank you very much for solving our problem.
